progress_bmi_info_body:
Body Mass Index (BMI) is a simple measure calculated from your weight and height. It helps estimate whether your weight falls within a healthy range.

Under 18.5 - Underweight
18.5-24.9 - Healthy
25-29.9 - Overweight
30 and above - Obese

BMI is a general guideline and does not account for muscle mass, bone density, or body composition.
progress_calendar_info_body:
This calendar shows your daily calorie intake for each day of the month.

Each day displays a colored ring that reflects how closely you met your calorie goal:

• Green — Within your target
• Red — Over your target
• Grey — No data recorded

Use the arrows to browse previous months.

onboarding_rollover_explanation:
If you eat under your goal, the unused calories will be added to tomorrow's budget.

If you eat over, the extra calories will be subtracted from tomorrow's budget.

personal_details_diet_title: Do you follow a specific diet?
personal_details_custom_diet_subtitle: Tell us about any food you don't eat, allergies, or dietary restrictions.
personal_details_activity_info_body:
If you plan to log your exercises manually or connect a health app, it is recommended to set your activity level to 0–1.

Otherwise, the calories burned from exercise may be counted twice - once through your activity level and again from logged workouts.
personal_details_bmr_info_body:
BMR is the number of calories your body burns at rest just to maintain basic life functions like breathing, circulation, and cell production.

We calculate it using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation based on your weight, height, age, and gender. Your activity level then determines your total daily energy expenditure (TDEE).

This will calculate your goals using the standard macro split:

30% protein, 40% carbs, 30% fats

Based on your weight, height, age, gender, and activity level.

fix_result_hint: Describe what needs to be fixed.
fix_result_example_text: The smoothie is missing the strawberry and almond milk.
fix_result_tip_body:
Describe what the AI got wrong so it can re-analyze your meal.

"It's grilled chicken, not fried."
"There's no cheese on the sandwich."
"The portion is about 300 g, not 150 g."
"Add a side of rice that's missing."
"The dressing is olive oil, not mayo."
fix_result_tip_subtitle: Tell the AI what it got wrong so it can re-analyze your meal.
fix_result_tip_cooking: Correct the cooking method: "It's grilled, not fried."
fix_result_tip_ingredient: Remove wrong items: "There's no cheese on the sandwich."
fix_result_tip_portion: Fix the portion size: "It's about 300 g, not 150 g."
fix_result_tip_missing: Add missing items: "Add a side of rice that's missing."
fix_result_tip_voice: You can also use the microphone to describe corrections by voice.
report_meal_hint: Tell us what is the issue with this meal log?
report_meal_example_text: This is chicken breast, not turkey. The portion is about 150g, not 250g.

streak_motivational: Every day matters for hitting your goal!
ring_colors_segment_info: The ring around each day shows how much of your daily calorie goal you've eaten.
ring_colors_segment_calc: The dark arc fills up proportionally — a half-filled ring means 50% of your goal.
ring_colors_segment_example: Example: 1500 / 2000 kcal → 75% → the ring is three-quarters full.
ring_colors_red_info: If you exceed 105% of your goal, a red arc appears showing how far over you are.
ring_colors_empty_desc: No records yet for this day.
faq_a_how_does_foody_work: It's simple: snap a photo, describe your meal in text or by voice, or scan a barcode. Foody analyzes the dish, estimates portion size and ingredients, and instantly shows you calories, protein, fat, and carbs. No manual calculations — just quick, effortless tracking in a couple of taps.
faq_q_how_accurate: How accurate is the calorie calculation?
faq_a_how_accurate:
Foody uses AI to estimate calories and macros (protein, fat, carbs). Accuracy depends on photo quality: good lighting, a clear top-down shot, and no extra objects in the frame help a lot.

For best results, take photos in daylight, close-up, and include a familiar object for scale — like a fork or your hand. This helps the AI better estimate portion size.
faq_q_estimate_off: What if the calorie estimate seems off?
faq_a_estimate_off: You can edit any meal after scanning. Tap the meal to adjust individual ingredients, quantities, or add missing items. If you want the AI to re-estimate, use the "Improve with AI" option — you can add a text description or a follow-up photo and the AI will generate a new estimate. Your corrections are saved immediately.
faq_a_per_serving: "Per serving" means the nutritional values shown are for one portion as estimated by the AI. If your plate has multiple servings, you can adjust the quantity in the edit screen.
faq_q_without_photo: Can I track meals without a photo?
faq_a_without_photo: Yes! You can describe your meal using text or voice input. The AI will estimate nutritional values from your description. You can also add meals manually or scan a barcode.
faq_a_barcode: Point your camera at a product barcode. Foody looks up the product in the Open Food Facts database and fills in the nutritional information automatically.
faq_q_nutrition_goals: How do I change my nutrition goals?
faq_a_nutrition_goals: Go to Profile and open the Nutrition Goals section. You can set custom targets for calories, protein, fat, and carbs. Goals apply from the current day forward.
faq_a_data_storage: No. All your data is stored locally on your device. Foody does not require an account or internet connection to view your history — a connection is only needed for AI analysis of meals. Optionally, you can export your data to CSV or PDF and import burned calories from Apple Health or Health Connect.
